examples: "He smiles deviously." Your colleagues may mention this in the media.
This was very limited but deviously powerful magic, for it enabled her to correct a just-made mistake.
An action calculated to frustrate an opponent or gain an advantage indirectly or deviously; a maneuver.
The latest charge:In several instances, Clear Channel is deviously using front companies to evade the Federal Communications Commission's strict ownership caps.
She created a series of captioned jpegs of him using memegenerator.net and then deviously search-optimized them to fill his results page.
